events: Dawn: Kaelith patrols seawall, Oathbrand flares unexpectedly
Dawn: Pallor tears overhead, reveals true starlight for ~3 breaths, seals
Mid-morning: Kaelith repairs garrison back wall
Mid-morning: Lirien watches from market square, makes herself visible
Afternoon: Kaelith confronts Tavik about surveillance, he lies transparently
Afternoon: Kaelith retreats to rented room, recognizes active containment
Dawn: Lirien observes Kaelith morning patrol from rooftop surveillance position
Morning: Kaelith works garrison wall, catches Lirien watching, both hold visual contact
Mid-morning: Kaelith confronts Tavik (observed by Lirien from distance)
Afternoon: Direct confrontation in market square, mutual assessment, Kaelith asks if Lirien is here to kill her
Afternoon: Lirien intercepts Seep intelligence packet revealing Pallor tear/Oathbrand correlation and Warden-lineage assessment
Evening: Lirien decides to delay execution to investigate paradox, has 48 hours before binding pain starts
Midnight: Kaelith breaks into Tavik office, reads encrypted Conclave messages
Midnight: Discovers Oathbrand deliberately maintained, Warden-lineage designation, prevention protocol
Dawn: Enforcer Seldric arrives with two subordinates, demands Kaelith transfer to Aranthem
Dawn: Lirien disrupts capture via scaffolding collapse, creates escape window
Morning: Kaelith and Lirien escape together, first tactical cooperation
Late morning: Abandoned warehouse discussion, Lirien reveals operative status
Late morning: Agreement to leave Duskhaven via smuggler boat (departs in 2 hours)
Pre-dawn: Lirien attempts to write operational report justifying scaffolding intervention, fails to construct valid rationale
Pre-dawn: Realizes she has 48 hours before binding pain starts, 5 days before termination
Morning: Lirien waits in garrison tavern for Kaelith
Morning: Kaelith confronts Lirien, asks directly why she has not been killed yet
Morning: Lirien reveals kill contract assessment, admits execution no longer being pursued
Morning: Lirien reveals Pallor being deliberately sabotaged for 40 years, Ythara waking threat
Morning: Lirien explains Kaelith has Warden-lineage capacity and kinetic memory of Quietus path
Morning: Kaelith learns she walked Quietus path blindfolded once (does not consciously remember)
Late morning: Kaelith returns after 3 hours, asks about Oathbrand removal possibility
Late morning: Both agree to board smuggler boat departing in 4 hours, names exchanged
Midnight: Kaelith reads Commander Helric's personal journal (YK 704-709 Pallor failure reports)
Midnight: Discovers 15 years of systematic suppression by Regional Command
Midnight: Journal ends mid-entry. Helric's fate unknown
Pre-dawn: Lirien arrives at Kaelith's quarters for confrontation
Pre-dawn: Confirms active contract; Kaelith is map to Quietus via kinetic memory
Pre-dawn: Paradox articulated: executing Kaelith = seal lost; keeping alive = Lirien dies
Dawn: Kaelith decides to cooperate. 'The numbers say: go'
Dawn: Departure preparations begin
Dawn Day 3: Kaelith and Lirien depart Duskhaven via eastern gate
Dawn Day 3: Tavik confronts them with two garrison guards, attempts to detain Kaelith per Conclave orders
Dawn Day 3: Lirien and Kaelith disable guards non-lethally, force Tavik to stand aside
Morning-afternoon Day 3: Travel east on Thornway, 12-hour forced march toward Veldrane
Evening Day 3: Make camp in oak-lined section of Thornway, shared watch through night
Day 4 morning: Lirien files misleading report to Seep during Kaelith brief sleep
Day 4: Continue travel toward Veldrane
Afternoon: Thornway embankment collapse. 35+ feet rock face to traverse
Afternoon: Joint climbing. Kaelith's alpine training + Lirien's vertical access
Afternoon: Kaelith reveals blindfolded climbing training (connects to kinetic memory)
Evening: Camp in forest clearing. Lirien bandages Kaelith's hand. Contact lingers
Night: Mutual attraction becomes explicit. Distance 'collapsing'
Arrival at Veldrane outer settlements
Marin (frontier healer) diagnoses: Oathbrand actively refreshed for 6 years
Diagnosis: magical pressure through stress fractures. 4-6 month mortality without removal
Removal requires original control key OR ward-breaker with Imperial expertise
Evening: Both process mortality timeline. Mission now has medical deadline
Lodging at common house in Veldrane
Morning: Depart Veldrane. Enter the Wending (damaged Pallor zone)
Morning: Pallor degradation 8-12 months ahead of projections
Morning: Kaelith exposed to Ythara influence for 43 seconds. Identity fragmentation
Morning: Lirien anchors Kaelith through voice and physical contact
Afternoon: Three Conclave agents attack at waystation
Afternoon: Instinctive combat coordination (pre-bond tactical synchronization)
Afternoon: Waystation documents reference Thalen Reyvas Seventh Seat
Evening: Camp at waystation ruin
Noon: Lirien infiltrates Aranthem municipal archive (7-min window)
Afternoon: 40-year conspiracy confirmed. Dimming rites weakening Pallor
Afternoon: Authorization chain to Thalen Reyvas Seventh Seat
Afternoon: 'Not sabotage by neglect. This is engineering.'
Evening: Shares findings with Kaelith (withholds third page)
Evening: First personal vulnerability exchange. Fae stargazing memories
Night: Kaelith practices tier-two magic. Rebuilding capacity
Arrival at Sanctuary. Meet Tessen Vael (ward-breaker, former Legion)
Oathbrand removal procedure. 7 layers, 3-4 hours. Lirien's fae precision magic
Final layer removal: six years suppressed magic floods back
Kaelith discovers compass-pull toward Quietus (kinetic memory activation)
RESONANCE BOND FORMS. Both feel emotional states continuously
Tessen identifies kinetic memory as 'Warden-work'
Pre-dawn: Continue northeast following Quietus pull
Morning: Lirien retrieves Seep dead drop. 40-year sabotage confirmed
Morning: 'Operative Dael flagged as deviation risk per Court advisory'
Afternoon: Four hunter teams attack. Suppression field deployed
Afternoon: Bond-enabled combat synchronization. Non-verbal coordination
Afternoon: Kaelith hit to left ribs. Pain echoes through bond
Evening: Escape through drainage channel. Highland meadow camp
Night: Lirien shares Vael Seren story. First genuine personal disclosure
Night: Kaelith and Lirien establish temporary camp on granite shelf after fleeing ch12 hunters
Night: Kaelith's agitation radiates through resonance bond — Calwrath memories surfacing
Night: Lirien asks directly what Kaelith is afraid of; Kaelith decides to tell the truth
Night: Calwrath confession — full unsanitized truth. 8 minutes, 300 civilians, children in market, magical accelerant, temple collapse
Night: Kaelith admits she would make the same call again — her deepest fear
Night: Lirien responds with witness, not absolution. Shares her own operation 31 (merchant in Vael'thrin, daughter Elisi)
Night: First kiss — deliberate, not desperate. Both acknowledge it as choice, not necessity
Pre-dawn: Both remain at camp, intending to move before dawn
Dawn: Coordinated ambush by four of Thalen's hunters — three attackers plus one suppression field anchor operator
Dawn: Suppression field specifically designed to counter Lirien's fae capabilities — triangulated resonance dampening
Dawn: Bond-enabled combat synchronization — Lirien and Kaelith fight as coordinated unit without verbal communication
Dawn: Kaelith takes hit to left ribs (third and fourth, bruised/hairline-cracked) — Lirien feels pain through bond
Dawn: Four attackers neutralized — one unconscious, one disarmed, one bound, one disabled by Kaelith
Post-dawn: Post-combat physical intimacy — first full intimate scene, freely chosen
Morning: Post-intimacy conversation — Kaelith asks about the future, Lirien answers with Vael Seren harbour story
Morning: Lirien discovers contract loophole — 'cessation of heartbeat' satisfies execution without permanent death (FACT-008)
Morning: Lirien commits to solving contract through loophole without confessing to Kaelith
Morning: Both depart northeast toward Quietus — Kaelith reports pull is stronger
Afternoon: Kaelith and Lirien arrive at safe house near the Narrowing (stone shelter, pre-empire construction)
Afternoon: Evidence consolidation — cross-referencing archived formulas, freeholder testimonies, Seep intelligence
Afternoon: Four independent sources corroborate Thalen Reyvas's 40-year Dimming rite sabotage (Veldrane, Rivenmarch, Serath Dûn, Seep intelligence)
Afternoon: Thalen's zealotry motivation confirmed — believes Ythara's waking will correct world stagnation
Evening: Lirien encodes evidence summary into Seep-compatible format as insurance
Evening: Kaelith and Lirien's emotional declaration — 'I want this. I want you.' — desire named without tactical framing
Evening: Lirien drops all operative bond filters in response — Kaelith feels uncontained emotion
Night: Future-talk — Vael Seren, harbour, life after mission
Night: Kaelith deliberately chooses hope despite recognising the Calwrath pattern
Night: Both fall asleep in safe house. Oathbrand phantom ache unusually quiet
Tomorrow: Narrowing crossing begins (3-day traverse)
Pre-dawn: Lirien slips out to check perimeter, finds Seep dead drop serviced
Pre-dawn: Intercepted Court communique reveals active kill contract — blood binding, physiological enforcement, 47-day overdue, enforcement escalation authorized
Dawn: Bond transmits Lirien's distress before she can control it, waking Kaelith
Dawn: Kaelith asks what's wrong, Lirien cannot lie through the bond
Dawn: Lirien hands over the intercepted message — bond has already transmitted enough that concealment is another lie
Dawn: Kaelith reads the contract — trust architecture collapses through the bond
Dawn: Kaelith processes the full scope — blood ceremony, active binding, every shared moment recontextualized
Dawn: Kaelith delivers 'Every feeling was built on a lie' in military casualty-report register
Dawn: Bond goes cold — walled by Kaelith, not severed
Dawn: Kaelith packs with military efficiency, leaving shared items behind
Dawn: Lirien attempts to explain loophole (cessation of heartbeat) — every word sounds like manipulation
Dawn: Kaelith's thesis: 'And you chose not to tell me'
Dawn: Kaelith departs alone into Narrowing treeline heading east
Dawn: Lirien does not follow — cannot determine if desire to follow is genuine or contract-driven
Morning: Lirien remains at safe house in paralysis. Bond carries matched grief across distance

timeline.day: Evening through next morning, ~1.5 days after ch16
events: Evening: Kaelith makes solo camp in rock overhang shelter in the Narrowing treeline, granite slopes. Fire built, perimeter established.
Evening: Pallor progression tracked — silver at wrist spreading to first knuckle. Cold deepening. Timeline mentally revised from weeks to days.
Evening: Bond walled but grief bleeding through — Kaelith feels Lirien's uncontrolled grief. Catalogs every shared memory as potential manipulation.
Night: Calwrath nightmare — Graymarket fire reimagined with silver flames and Pallor imagery.
Night: Bond pulse — Lirien's grief breaches wall. Physical impact. Kaelith wakes, processes bond evidence.
Night: Kaelith works through bond logic — resonance cannot fabricate emotion, therefore Lirien's grief is genuine, therefore simple betrayal narrative fails.
Night: Stops actively reinforcing bond wall. Frames as tactical monitoring. Self-deception acknowledged.
Dawn: Packs camp. Silver spread to tendons on back of left hand, cold to elbow. Half-second reaction delay in fingers.
Morning: Walks east three hours. Kinetic memory guiding toward Quietus.

Morning: Vorakh encounter — Pallor-touched predator, six limbs, ring-eyes. Kaelith fights alone. Left arm locks mid-combat (Pallor paralysis). Bruised ribs (ch14) aggravated in fall. Kills vorakh but barely — 90 seconds instead of usual 30.
Morning: Post-combat assessment — two fingers non-functional, silver at wrist inside/knuckles outside. Solo mission completion no longer viable.
Morning: Changes direction from east to north — toward Quietus AND Lirien convergence. Bond registers the shift. Lirien's grief gains edge of alertness.
Morning: Walking north, framing return as mission necessity. Self-deception transparent.

timeline.day: Parallel with Ch17 — second day after Ch16 separation, dawn through afternoon
events: Dawn: Lirien has been awake ~11 hours conducting systematic self-interrogation at the safe house table. Repurposed accounting ledger for operational notation. Every decision since contract inception classified.
Dawn: Systematic review of all decisions — each classified as 'ambiguous' due to binding-compatible explanations. Analytical death-spiral: the more entries produced, the more ambiguity compounds.
Dawn: Bond pulse felt from Kaelith (parallel to Ch17 night breach). Grief quality changes — something happened to Kaelith that Lirien cannot read through the wall.

Dawn: Reaches Oathbrand removal decision (Ch11 at Tessen's shelter). Binding directive: PREVENT. Actual action: performed. Three hours direct magical contact. Binding resistance: NONE. Classification: free choice, confidence high.
Dawn: Breakthrough — one verified act of free choice proves capacity for choice exists within binding. Foundation for further decisions.

Midday: Ashborne contact arrives at safe house. Male, middle years, former soldier of Kaelith's Ashborne Fourth. Uses pre-Court neutral intelligence recognition protocol. Does not enter until invited.

Midday: Intelligence exchange — Ashborne contact reveals: blood-sealed binding architecture has vulnerability at metaphysical substrate thin-points. God-seal at Quietus exposes anchor structure. Dissolution requires both Kaelith (Warden capacity, kinetic memory) and Lirien (binding expertise, precision magic).
Midday: Three intelligence sources cited: defected Court Architect (12 years in Ashborne protection), Pallor scholar at Serath Dûn, field operative who surveyed Quietus approach.

Midday: Lirien verifies through three channels: defection story against Seep archive memory, binding architecture claim against personal expertise, physical tells for deception. Provisional acceptance.
Midday: Contact reveals Ashborne network has been watching Kaelith's exile for years. Kaelith does not know they exist. 'She thinks she's alone. She's not.'

Afternoon: Lirien destroys Seep encryption cipher. Threadbone material broken at designed stress point. Encoding matrix separates from substrate. Identity severance: Classification: unnecessary. Status: destroyed. New classification: Lirien Dael, unaffiliated.
Afternoon: Lirien packs and walks north from safe house into the Narrowing. Bond registers direction change — intention transmitted to Kaelith. Walking toward Quietus AND Kaelith by free choice.
Afternoon: Bond carries intention north. Both walking same direction for the first time since separation.
Kaelith arrives at Rivenmarch freeholder settlement
Discovers Ashborne veterans (8+) organized using her tactical doctrine
Greeted as 'Commander' by Sedara (Third Platoon veteran)
Bond signals Lirien's approach — quality shifted from grief to intention
Kaelith chooses to stay and face Lirien rather than flee
Lirien arrives with destroyed cipher, Ashborne intelligence, proof of choice
Confrontation: Lirien presents one verified act of free choice (Oathbrand removal)
Kaelith names deepest fear — choosing love as becoming blast radius
Reconciliation through decision, not forgiveness — bond opens fully
Strategy session: god-seal dissolution plan established (primary path)
Heartbeat cessation acknowledged as fallback mechanism
Three-day travel plan to Quietus via Ashborne relay points
Kaelith agrees to trust Lirien with contract dissolution
Lirien and Kaelith arrive at forward shelter (Ashborne relay network) between Rivenmarch and Quietus
Lirien sets ward-lattice — sealed ritual space for contract loophole execution
Timing calculations: 4-7 second window for binding to register cessation, 12-second resuscitation window
Kaelith gives explicit verbal consent for heartbeat cessation procedure
Lirien initiates fae magic through resonance bond targeting cardiac conduction
Binding enforcement mechanism resists — fights the loophole for ~3 seconds
Kaelith's heart stops. Bond goes silent. Binding registers fulfillment after 4 seconds
Contract architecture dissolves in cascade of energy — blood-sealed binding unravels from Lirien's body
Bond silence lasts ~8 seconds total — the absence is devastating
Lirien channels everything through bond — magic, will, love — and restarts Kaelith's heart
Bond floods back with overwhelming intensity — cleaner without binding interference
Lirien catalogues binding absence — constant spine pressure gone, directional pull gone, emotional interference gone
Seep tacit support for loophole acknowledged — contract language always contained exploitable phrasing (FS-011)
Love confirmed: feelings unchanged after binding removal. Proof complete (FS-012 resolved, MB-008 resolved, MB-012 resolved)
Kaelith redirects to mission — Quietus still three days out, Thalen still operating, god-seal needs reinforcement
Partnership continues by choice, not contract obligation
timeline.location: Forward shelter, Ashborne relay network, between Rivenmarch and Quietus

characterPositions.CHAR-001: Forward shelter between Rivenmarch and Quietus
characterPositions.CHAR-002: Forward shelter between Rivenmarch and Quietus (same location)

events: Descent into Serath Dun corridors (pre-empire, 2000+ years old)
Kaelith learns Ossya Thenndal planted kinetic memory 12 years ago
Enter Quietus chamber. Seal cracked with Ythara's influence leaking
Confrontation with Thalen Reyvas at seal
Thalen admits 40-year corruption. Believes waking Ythara will save world
Combat: Lirien deflects Thalen while Kaelith works seal
WARDEN MAGIC: Kaelith channels conviction. Cracks slow, stop, close
Pallor silver on forearm thins slightly after seal work
Thalen neutralized. Evidence entered with witnesses
Public tribunal ordered. Kaelith refuses execution
Dawn: Lirien wakes at shelter base of Serath Dún — first morning without binding architecture
Dawn: Lirien catalogues changed world — Pallor thinner but stable, ambient magic accessible, landscape altered
Morning: Ashborne scout delivers situation report — Pallor holding, Conclave in emergency session, freeholders adapting, Thalen secure
Midmorning: Conclave relay response arrives — acknowledges Thalen's crimes, hedges on Kaelith's exile reversal, 'established administrative channels'
Midmorning: Lirien presents Conclave response to Kaelith — Kaelith responds with 'I stopped needing their permission when I started needing something real'
Afternoon: Allies disperse — Sedara leads Ashborne veterans escorting Thalen south for public tribunal, Tessen Vael carries evidence, Iryse remains reviewing Dimming rites
Late afternoon: Kaelith and Lirien alone for first time without crisis — discuss future, Rivenmarch as destination
Late afternoon: Lirien declares 'I stay because I want to' — HFN resolution, love chosen freely
Evening: Final image — held hands, bond humming, first real star visible through thinned Pallor
timeline.location: Shelter at the base of Serath Dún, near the Quietus

characterPositions.CHAR-001: Serath Dún shelter, planning return to Rivenmarch
characterPositions.CHAR-002: Serath Dún shelter, choosing to stay with Kaelith

CHAR-001.location: Shelter at the base of Serath Dún, planning return to Rivenmarch
CHAR-001.status: Post-climax recovery. Silver on left forearm permanent — three fingers frozen, Pallor claim on body from seal reinforcement. Combat-capable but carrying permanent physical cost. Emotionally: hard-won peace, need for institutional vindication outgrown, focused on building rather than fighting.

CHAR-002.location: Shelter at the base of Serath Dún, choosing to remain with Kaelith
CHAR-002.status: Kill contract dissolved. Binding fully removed. Left shoulder burn from Thalen's ward — healing. Fae magic fully available. Bond intact, clean, chosen. No institutional affiliation — cipher destroyed, Court jurisdiction dissolved. Emotionally: chosen love, identity question resolved through daily decision rather than classification.

characters.CHAR-002: Lirien Dael, Lirien (named in Kaelith's POV since ch4)
characters.Tarris: Shaper on southeastern battery at Calwrath, female, served with Kaelith for two years
characters.Tessen: Tessen Vael, Rivenmarch freeholder guide, present at Oathbrand removal in ch11
characters.Thalen Reyvas: High Conclave member, antagonist. NOTE: world-bible/politics.json says Sixth Seat (Fae Relations), but chapters 10/12/13 consistently use Seventh Seat. Cross-chapter discrepancy requires global resolution.
characters.Elisi: Daughter (age 7) of merchant killed by Lirien in operation 31 in Vael'thrin
characters.Enforcer Seldric: Conclave enforcer. Arrives ch3 with 2 subordinates. Not seen after ch3.
characters.Marin: Frontier healer, former military medic. First appears ch8 Veldrane. No surname.
characters.Commander Helric: Previous Duskhaven commander. Journal found ch3/ch5. Disappeared ~10 years prior.
characters.Sedara: Ashborne Third Platoon veteran. Coordinates network at Rivenmarch. First appears ch19.
characters.Iryse: Dimming rites keeper. Unknowing sabotage vector. Present at Quietus ch21.
characters.Ossya Thenndal: Last Warden. Deceased. Planted kinetic memory in Kaelith 12 years ago.
characters.Ythara the Unfinished: Imprisoned divinity. God of unmade things. Contained by Quietus seal.

locations.Graymarket: Civilian district in Calwrath, site of the burn. Spelling: Graymarket (one word, capital G)
locations.Canal District: Calwrath defensive line along reinforced stone embankments
locations.Vael'thrin: City where Lirien's operation 31 occurred (apostrophe between l and t)
locations.The Quietus: God-seal destination, northeast of current position
locations.Thornway: Major northeast imperial road. Departure route from Duskhaven ch6.
locations.Veldrane: Frontier settlement, ~40 miles south. Marin's location. First visited ch8.
locations.The Wending: Damaged Pallor section. Reality unstable. Ythara influence. Ch9.
locations.The Narrowing: Mountain pass region. Relay waypoints. Pallor thinning. Vorakh habitat.
locations.Serath Dun: Pre-empire construction, 2000+ years. Contains Quietus chamber.
locations.Rivenmarch: Freeholder settlement. Ashborne veterans. Future home destination.
locations.Vael Seren: Harbour in southern Cerith territories. Lirien's dream destination.
locations.Vireth Range: Mountain system containing Quietus / Serath Dun.

magicTerms.Oathbrand: Consistent — disciplinary brand that suppresses magic. Removed in ch11.
magicTerms.Pallor: Consistent — divine membrane, tarnished silver, 'flayed skin of a god'
magicTerms.Shapers: Magic practitioners (mentioned in context of brand maintenance and Calwrath accelerant)
magicTerms.resonance bond: Formed ch11 during Oathbrand removal. Transmits emotions and physical sensations. Does NOT transmit thoughts, words, or factual information. Both parties feel each other simultaneously. Can be walled (blocked) by deliberate emotional withdrawal — bond persists but warmth blocked. Wall is a choice, not a failure (ch16).
magicTerms.accelerant: Military-grade magical accelerant — does not spread like natural fire, 'arrives everywhere at once'
magicTerms.Lessened / Lessening: State resulting from binding contract violation or non-completion. Progressive deterioration culminating in ontological collapse — 'an absence wearing the shape of what had been removed.' Not a creature. A state.
magicTerms.Hand of the Third Reach: Lirien's formal Cerith Court rank/designation. Appears in Court administrative contract document (ch16). Not previously referenced — new naming entry.
magicTerms.Ashborne Fourth: Kaelith's specific unit within the Ashborne Legion. Formal designation used in Court administrative documents (ch16). Colloquial references use 'Ashborne Legion' throughout.
magicTerms.vorakh: Pallor-touched predator found in the Narrowing. Six limbs, hooked claws, compressed wolf-head with ring-eyes (full spherical vision), thick plated hide with Pallor silver seepage. Ambush predator that hunts by sensing Pallor-touch in prey. First introduced ch17.
magicTerms.kinetic memory: Body-encoded knowledge through movement. Cannot be interrogated. Warden-work.
magicTerms.Dimming rites: Pallor maintenance ceremonies. Corrupted by Thalen over 40 years.
magicTerms.Warden magic: Ontological-grade. Reality structural interface. Conviction not force.
magicTerms.Seep: Fae intelligence org, Court-affiliated. Lirien's network. Cipher destroyed ch18.
magicTerms.suppression field: Triangulated resonance dampening. Counters fae magic ~60%.
magicTerms.ward-stones: Magical containment elements for sealed ritual spaces.

openDiscrepancies.description: Thalen Reyvas seat number: world-bible/politics.json assigns Sixth Seat. ALL chapter drafts use Seventh Seat consistently. Seventh Seat in politics.json belongs to Vairen Ethis. Recommend updating politics.json.
Binding timeline ambiguity Ch2 vs Ch4. Approximate consistency but exact arithmetic is ambiguous across different baseline references.

Character ID mapping error: info-asymmetry-map uses CHAR-014 for Thalen Reyvas, but characters/index.json assigns CHAR-003 to Thalen and CHAR-014 to Daerin Morweth. All CHAR-014 references describing Thalen should be CHAR-003.
FACT-005 dramatic irony: activeFromChapter:5, resolvedChapter:4 is temporally impossible. Should be activeFrom:3, resolved:4.
Multiple FACT entries have stale unknownBy lists not updated for ch19-22. FACT-006, FACT-007, FACT-011, FACT-016 all have outdated knowledge tracking.
Foreshadowing ledger: duplicate IDs FS-017 through FS-020 each appear twice with different content.
Foreshadowing ledger: many entries show status 'planted' despite having recorded payoff data.
Mystery box inventory: activeCount says 15 but all boxes resolved by ch22. Should be 0.

notes: CROSS-MANUSCRIPT VERIFICATION COMPLETE. Full 22-chapter pass. Timeline gaps filled (ch 5,7,8,9,10,11,12,21). POV alternation verified. 8 discrepancies: DISC-001 (Reyvas seat, MAJOR), DISC-002 (binding timeline, minor), DISC-003 (CHAR-014/003 ID error, MAJOR), DISC-004 (FACT-005 temporal, minor), DISC-005 (stale unknownBy, moderate), DISC-006 (duplicate FS IDs, minor), DISC-007 (stale FS status, minor), DISC-008 (mystery box count, minor). Magic system verified: no violations. Bond mechanics consistent. Contract loophole ch20 consistent with MAG-005 and FU-001. Character positions verified: no teleportation. All promises resolved.
